---
title: "group_1_part_2"
output: html_document
date: "2025-05-29"
---

Top-20 F1 scores across SAE layers

```{r setup, message = FALSE}
library(tidyverse)   # includes dplyr, ggplot2, readr, purrr, stringr, magrittr
library(ggforce)     # geom_sina
```

Locating the CSV files
```{r}
base_dir <- "/Volumes/T7 Shield/group_1_F1_scores"

csv_files_layers <- list.files(
  path       = base_dir,
  pattern    = "winning_thresholds_layer_\\d+\\.csv$",
  recursive  = TRUE,
  full.names = TRUE
)

```

Read and tag each row with its layer number
```{r}
f1_all_layers_df <- csv_files_layers %>%
  set_names() %>%                                    # keep filenames as names
  map_dfr(read_csv, show_col_types = FALSE, .id = "file") %>%
  mutate(layer = str_extract(file, "(?<=layer_)\\d+") %>%     # digits only
  as.integer()) %>%
  select(-file)
```

Keep the 20 strongest F1 scores per layer
```{r}
top_df <- f1_all_layers_df %>%
  group_by(layer) %>%
  slice_max(winning_F1_score, n = 100, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  ungroup()
```

Violin + sina plot
```{r}
ggplot(
  top_df,
  aes(
    x = factor(layer, levels = sort(unique(layer))),
    y = winning_F1_score
  )
) +
  geom_violin(fill = "#7EC8E3", colour = "grey40", scale = "width") +
  geom_sina(size = 0.6, color = "#1D3557", alpha = 1, scale = "width") +
  labs(
    x = "SAE layer",
    y = "F1 score (top 100 per layer)",
    title = "distribution of highest F1 scores by layer"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    plot.title.position = "plot"
  )
```

```{r}
ggplot(
  f1_all_layers_df,
  aes(
    x = factor(layer, levels = sort(unique(layer))),
    y = winning_accuracy
  )
) +
  geom_violin(fill = "#7EC8E3", colour = "grey40", scale = "width") +
  geom_sina(size = 1.6, colour = "#1D3557", alpha = 0.8, scale = "width") +
  labs(
    x = "SAE layer",
    y = "Accuracy across all features",
    title = "Distribution of accuracy by layer"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    plot.title.position = "plot"
  )
```

Note: winning thresholds are all zero
